cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a donut and coffee from this highly loved vegan cafe on Santa Clara St — the donuts are the real draw, so grab a couple and take your time.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so hit it early before the good stuff sells out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition on your way through Santa Clara — it's a visually interesting show worth at least an hour of your afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, and daytime-only so now is the time to catch it.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and just pick whatever looks good — tacos, banh mi, ramen, it's all under one roof and you can grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown with no wrong answer on what to order.

cards.name: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
cards.blurb: Head to Hammer Theatre for the SJSU Opera Theatre double bill — La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator are two very different pieces and together they make for a genuinely interesting afternoon of live performance.
cards.why: It's free and it's live opera in a great venue, which is a rare afternoon treat.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge, where you pick a piece, paint it however you want, and they fire it for you — genuinely fun for adults and a great low-key way to end the afternoon.
cards.why: It's creative, relaxed, and a solid way to decompress after a full day out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Wander through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — the kind of visual art show that messes with your perception in the best way.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely interesting way to spend a cool morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and check out Fists of Fury, an exhibition digging into the history and culture of martial arts in America.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and the kind of thing you'd never stumble on otherwise.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from a dozen vendors — tacos, ramen, banh mi, whatever sounds good. Snag a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: It's the most fun way to do lunch downtown when you can't agree on one thing.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Bowl a few frames at the SJSU Student Union Bowling Center. It's a real lanes setup, not a pop-up, and it's open to the public.
cards.why: Low-key competitive and way more fun than it sounds on a afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row in the early evening, browse the boutiques, and find a table at one of the outdoor restaurants when you're ready to sit down.
cards.why: The boulevard is relaxed on a weekday evening and the patio vibes are solid.

cards.name: LUNA Mexican Kitchen - San Jose
cards.address: 1495 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with dinner at LUNA Mexican Kitchen on The Alameda. Order margaritas and something off the dinner menu and call it a good.
cards.why: Solid Mexican food with a vibe that doesn't feel rushed.

Monday, April 20 — here's a whole day plan across Mountain View and Santa Clara, and it actually flows ✨

First stop is The Escape Game in Santa Clara — good way to wake up your brain before the caffeine kicks in. Then head over to the Computer History Museum and let yourself wander through the full arc of computing history. It's one of those places where two hours disappear fast.

Lunch is at Next Level Board Game Cafe — order food, pull a game off their shelf, and settle in. No rush. After that, Stevens Creek Trail is the perfect reset: flat, easy, and just scenic enough to feel like you actually went outside 🌿

Late afternoon at California's Great America if you're ready to pick up the energy again, and dinner at The Stand on Lawrence Expressway — elevated American classics that earn the detour.

item.title: From Bestseller to Classic: What Makes a Book Endure?
item.name: From Bestseller to Classic: What Makes a Book Endure?
item.summary: Why do some books become classics? Two book buffs join us to explore why certain novels stand the test of time. Elizabeth Rosner is joined in lively conversation with veteran critic John McMurtrie (formerly of the San Francisco Chronicle ) about what makes certain novels resonate with readers over time.

copy.x: Tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto: why do some books become classics while others fade? Elizabeth Rosner and critic John McMurtrie dig into that question at 6 PM. Worth the drive. https://www.keplers.org/upcoming-events-internal/classic-books
copy.threads: Tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto, two people who've spent their lives thinking about books sit down to ask the big question: why do some novels last forever while others disappear? Elizabeth Rosner and veteran critic John McMurtrie (formerly of the SF Chronicle) get into it at 6 PM. Free, smart, and the kind of conversation you'll keep having on the drive home. 📚 https://www.keplers.org/upcoming-events-internal/classic-books #PaloAlto #BookLovers #SouthBay
copy.bluesky: Tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto — Elizabeth Rosner and SF Chronicle critic John McMurtrie tackle the question: what actually makes a book a classic? 6 PM, and it's the kind of talk that'll rearrange your reading list. #BookLovers #PaloAlto
copy.facebook: If you love books and have ever wondered why certain novels stick around for centuries while others vanish, tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto is worth your evening. Author Elizabeth Rosner sits down with veteran critic John McMurtrie — formerly of the San Francisco Chronicle — to dig into exactly that question. It starts at 6 PM and sounds like the kind of conversation that's genuinely hard to leave. https://www.keplers.org/upcoming-events-internal/classic-books
copy.instagram:
Tonight's pick for the South Bay 📚

Kepler's Books in Palo Alto is hosting one of those conversations that sounds simple on the surface — why do some books become classics? — but gets surprisingly deep once you're in the room.

Author Elizabeth Rosner is joined by John McMurtrie, a veteran literary critic who spent years at the San Francisco Chronicle, and together they're going to pull apart what makes certain novels resonate with readers across generations while others quietly disappear.

It starts at 6 PM. If you've ever finished a book and thought "this one's going to matter for a long time" — or picked up a so-called classic and wondered why anyone bothered — this is your crowd.

Kepler's has been running conversations like this for decades, and they tend to feel more like a great dinner party discussion than a lecture. Come with opinions.

cards.blurb: Walk or bike the loop around Shoreline Lake, take in the bay views, and enjoy the open space while the morning is still cool and calm.
cards.why: 75 degrees and sunny is basically the ideal Shoreline day.

cards.name: SWEET55 - Swiss Chocolates & Confections
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real Suite 123, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into SWEET55 and pick up a few Swiss chocolates or confections to snack on while you figure out lunch. Small shop, big quality.
cards.why: A quick treat stop that punches well above its size.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Top-rated arts spot in mountain-view.

cards.name: Euro Market
cards.address: 980 El Camino Real #300, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Euro Market's impressive selection of European imports, specialty cheeses, charcuterie, wines, and pantry items you won't find at a regular grocery store.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun place to wander and discover things to bring home.

cards.name: Cinemark Century Mountain View 16
cards.address: 1500 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Top-rated food spot in mountain-view.

We planned your Monday in Mountain View — and it's genuinely a great one 😊

Here's how the day goes:

☀️ 8:30 AM — Castro Street Farmers Market. Grab a coffee, pick up a snack, ease into the morning the right way.

🚴 10:00 AM — Shoreline Park & Lake. Walk or bike the loop, take in the bay views, enjoy some open air before the day fills up.

🍫 12:00 PM — SWEET55. Swing in for Swiss chocolates and confections. A little treat between stops never hurts.

🧀 4:00 PM — Euro Market. European imports, specialty cheeses, charcuterie, wines — great spot to pick up something for the week.

🎬 6:30 PM — Cinemark Century Mountain View 16. End the night with a movie. Easy, comfortable, perfect landing spot after a full day.

cards.name: Zareen's Palo Alto
cards.address: 365 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Top-rated food spot in palo-alto.

cards.name: Arastradero Creek Trail
cards.address: Arastradero Creek Trail, Palo Alto, CA 94304, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Arastradero Creek Trail while the temperature is still mild. It's a shaded, easy-going trail that winds through open space and feels nothing like the suburbs around it.
cards.why: 74 degrees and sunny is basically a mandate to be outside.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Swing through the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus and spend some time with the permanent collection and the Rodin Sculpture Garden out front. It's free, it's world-class, and the campus on is genuinely beautiful.
cards.why: Free art on a gorgeous day with no crowds to fight.

cards.name: Dish N Dash
cards.address: 20750 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Dish N Dash on Stevens Creek for a solid Middle Eastern lunch. Go for the shawarma, a mezze spread, or whatever's calling your name off the menu.
cards.why: It's consistently one of the better lunches in the South Bay.

cards.name: Magical Bridge Playground
cards.address: 3700 Middlefield Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Top-rated other spot in palo-alto.

cards.name: Shahi Darbar Indian Cuisine
cards.address: 2155 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Shahi Darbar on El Camino in Santa Clara. Order the butter chicken, a biryani, and some bread and settle in for a proper dinner.
cards.why: It's some of the best Indian food in the South Bay and the perfect way to close out a full day.

Full itinerary right here: https://southbaytoday.org/plan/9890070e
copy.instagram:
We planned your Tuesday — so you don't have to 🗓️

Here's how it goes:

☀️ Start at Zareen's in Palo Alto. One of the most-loved spots in the area, and a great way to kick off the day.

🌿 Head over to the Arastradero Creek Trail while the temperature is still mild. It's shaded, easy-going, and exactly what a Tuesday morning should feel like.

🎨 Swing through the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. The permanent collection alone is worth the stop — and it won't cost you anything.

🥙 Lunch at Dish N Dash on Stevens Creek in Cupertino. Go for the shawarma or build out a mezze spread. Solid Middle Eastern, every time.

🛝 Afternoon stop at Magical Bridge Playground in Palo Alto — one of the best all-abilities playgrounds in the Bay Area. Bring the kids or just take a walk through.

🍛 End the night at Shahi Darbar on El Camino in Santa Clara. Order the butter chicken, a biryani, and don't skip the bread. A warm, filling finish to a full day.

Full itinerary at https://southbaytoday.org/plan/9890070e

Tonight's pick 📚 If you haven't discovered Jesse Q. Sutanto yet, this is a great place to start — and if you already love her, you already know what to do.

Jesse Q. Sutanto, the USA Today bestselling author of Vera Wong's Unsolicited Advice for Murderers, is at Kepler's Books in Palo Alto tonight at 7 PM. She'll be in conversation with Stacey Lee, talking about her sharp, witty new book Ms. Mebel Goes Back to the Chopping Block. 🩸😄

Kepler's is one of the best independent bookstores in the Bay Area, and their author events are genuinely fun — not just a reading, but a real conversation. Worth the drive.
